seoTitle: Mont Albert vs Montgomery — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mont Albert and Montgomery. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mont Albert vs Montgomery: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mont Albert and Montgomery.
heading: Mont Albert vs Montgomery — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mont Albert, Victoria and Montgomery, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mont Albert has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Montgomery (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mont Albert or Montgomery safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mont Albert has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Montgomery scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mont Albert scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Montgomery scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mont Albert or Montgomery?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mont Albert has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.77% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
